rationale: Postcondition is unprovable without forbidden axioms. det_clone_up_to_view_equal(r1,r2) := (r1.id == r2.id) requires SMT equality on Vec<u8>. The premise (r1@==self_@ && r2@==self_@) only yields view (Seq) equality r1.id@ == r2.id@. Vec is external_type_specification/external_body in vstd and has no #[verifier::ext_equal], so two Vec<u8> with identical Seq views can be SMT-distinct. Empirically verified: 'requires a@==b@ ensures a==b' (and even '=~=') fails on Vec<u8>. lemma_vec_obeys_view_eq only links view equality to eq_spec (PartialEq's spec relation), not SMT '=='. None of vstd::std_specs::vec::group_vec_axioms bridge view equality to '=='. No combination of conditional assumes in the body forces a contradiction in the all-g_*-false branch where r1, r2, self_ remain arbitrary. Hence A ==> B cannot be discharged in vanilla Verus. Matches the analogous parse_end_points artifact's prior failure under the same policy.
